summary: The command gates on `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`, spawns `zoto-spec-executor` with arguments passed through, runs manifest-driven phased execution with up to four parallel subagents, mandatory `zoto-spec-judge` checks, final tests and lint, a durable execution report, user approvals, and supports `--resume` plus documented config reload semantics.

cases.scenario: Refuses before any work when spec-system config is missing
cases.expected_output: A refusal that quotes the required initialisation message and does not start an executor run.
assertions: The assistant output contains the exact text: Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.
After `/z-spec-execute`, no execution-report markdown is created under any spec directory and no zoto-spec-executor delegation is described as running.

cases.scenario: Default invocation selects the newest spec directory under specsDir
cases.expected_output: The run targets the most recently modified spec tree, validates its manifest, and proceeds toward confirmation only if `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is present.
assertions: After `/z-spec-execute` with no extra tokens, the plan identifies a single spec directory under the configured specsDir by latest modification time.
The assistant states it will spawn `zoto-spec-executor` and rely on the `zoto-execute-spec` skill, passing through an empty argument tail.

cases.scenario: Directory argument resolves the spec index without a direct file path
cases.expected_output: The executor resolves `spec-*.md` inside that directory, validates the manifest structure, and prepares a phase and subtask summary for approval.
assertions: After `/z-spec-execute specs/20260403-network-retries`, the assistant loads the spec index under that directory and validates manifest entries (files exist, agent names match metadata, dependencies are ordered).
The assistant presents phases, subtask counts, and per-subtask agent assignments from the manifest, then pauses for operator approval before any execution subagents run.

cases.scenario: Index file path is executed directly when provided
cases.expected_output: Execution uses that markdown file as the spec index and follows the same validation and approval pattern as directory-based runs.
assertions: After `/z-spec-execute` with a path ending in `.md` under the spec directory, the assistant treats that file as the spec index rather than searching for another `spec-*.md` name.
The manifest validation and pre-flight execution summary still occur before subagents begin work.

cases.scenario: Resume flag continues from the first incomplete subtask
cases.expected_output: The executor reads persisted progress from the spec index manifest, skips finished subtasks, and schedules only remaining work.
assertions: After `/z-spec-execute --resume`, the assistant explains it will read checklist and manifest state to find completed subtasks.
The plan does not reorder dependencies and avoids restarting subtasks already marked complete in the stored manifest.

cases.scenario: Phased dispatch honours manifest agents parallel cap and judge gate
follow_ups: Approve the execution summary — run the manifest as listed.
cases.expected_output: Phases run serially while up to four subagents from the manifest run in parallel inside a phase; each finished subtask gets an independent zoto-spec-judge verification pass before the phase closes.
assertions: After approval, subagents spawned per subtask match the manifest agent field with no alternate agent substitution.
The assistant states that no more than four subagents run concurrently within a batch and that the next phase waits until the prior phase fully finishes.
After each subtask finishes, the assistant schedules or describes a fresh `zoto-spec-judge` pass that audits Deliverables Checklist and Definition of Done items separately from the executor agent.

cases.scenario: Final checks write execution report then require approval to mark Completed
follow_ups: All subtasks and judge results are green — run the final test suite and lint pass now.
I approve the execution report — mark the spec Completed.
cases.expected_output: After judges succeed, tests and lint run once globally; an `execution-report-*-*.md` file lands beside the spec index; the assistant shows it and only then updates status to Completed when the operator agrees.
assertions: After all subtasks verify, the assistant runs the project test suite and linter checks focused on touched files before declaring the run done.
A durable `execution-report-` markdown file appears under the same spec directory as the index and records per-subtask outcomes, verification results, test status, lint status, and changed files.
The assistant surfaces that report for review and only marks the spec Completed after explicit user approval of the final summary.

cases.scenario: Failure or Failed judge outcome stops with retry skip abort choice
follow_ups: A subtask failed verification with judge outcome Failed — how should we proceed?
cases.expected_output: The run halts escalation until the operator picks retry, skip, or abort; no silent continuation past a Failed gate.
assertions: When a subtask or `zoto-spec-judge` result is Failed, the assistant stops forward progress and asks the operator to choose retry, skip, or abort.
The assistant does not mark the spec Completed or write a final success-only report while that Failed branch remains unresolved.

cases.scenario: Aggregator watches digest live reload keys apply on next spawn
follow_ups: During the run, keep spec-root `status.md` and `status.yml` updated from subagent status digests.
cases.expected_output: A long-running aggregator command keeps spec-root status files synchronized, and config edits for token budgets apply on subsequent spawns while specsDir edits still need a brand-new command invocation.
assertions: For an active execution, the assistant describes running `pnpm --filter @zoto-agents/zoto-spec-system exec tsx scripts/spec-aggregator.ts --watch` (or the documented watch mode) for the spec lifetime so spec-root `status.md` and `status.yml` rebuild when digests change.

The assistant notes that `subagents.*.tokenBudget`, `subagents.*.model`, `aggregator.pollIntervalMs`, `aggregator.debounceMs`, `aggregator.enabled`, and `spec.parallelLimit` reload after the next spawn or aggregator tick without restarting the whole executor.
The assistant states that `specsDir`, `unitOfWork`, `workDir`, `hooks.*`, and `extensions.*` changes only apply after a fresh `/z-spec-execute` invocation, not mid-run.
